title: Hope & Motivation
lead: When everything feels stuck, waiting for motivation can keep you frozen. Often, action comes first — and one tiny step is enough to start.
explanation: Losing hope and motivation can feel like a personal failing, but it's usually a symptom — of exhaustion, depression, overwhelm, or repeated disappointment. When the tank is empty, 'just try harder' doesn't work, and shaming yourself makes it worse.

We often believe we need to feel motivated before we act. In reality it's frequently the reverse: taking a small action first creates a spark of momentum and mood that then fuels more action. Motivation follows movement as often as it precedes it.

Hope isn't naive optimism that everything will be fine. It's the belief that things can change and that your actions matter — and it can be rebuilt in small, concrete ways, especially with support and tiny wins.

immediate.title: Shrink it until it's easy
steps: Take whatever you 'should' do and cut it to something almost laughably small
Not 'go for a run' but 'put on my shoes'
Momentum often carries you further — but the tiny step counts on its own

immediate.title: Act first, feel second
immediate.description: Don't wait to feel like it. Move your body, start the smallest piece, and let a bit of motivation catch up to the action instead of the other way around.

immediate.title: Find one anchor of hope
immediate.description: Recall one time you got through something hard, or one small thing you're looking forward to. Hope is built from small evidence that things can shift.

longTerm.title: Behavioural activation
longTerm.description: A well-evidenced approach: gradually reintroducing small, meaningful, or pleasurable activities to rebuild energy, mood, and momentum.

longTerm.title: Connect actions to values
longTerm.description: Motivation lasts longer when it's tied to what genuinely matters to you, rather than what you think you 'should' do. Small acts aligned with your values rebuild purpose.

longTerm.title: Rule out and treat what's underneath
longTerm.description: Persistent hopelessness can be a sign of depression or burnout. Addressing those directly is often what restores real motivation.

resources.title: Behavioural activation explained
resources.whyHere: Free, clear therapist videos on rebuilding motivation and beating the freeze.

resources.title: How to overcome procrastination
resources.whyHere: Free, research-based strategies for getting unstuck.

resources.title: The Happiness Trap (free resources)
resources.whyHere: Free ACT worksheets on values and committed action.

resources.title: Healthy Minds Program
resources.whyHere: Free training with a 'purpose' pillar that helps rebuild direction.

matchPhrases: i feel hopeless about the future
i don't see the point in trying
